Ad Text:
Lead critical business readiness activities as the organisation transitions from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA.

Your new company

Our client, a major government department, is embarking on a significant finance transformation project. This initiative will upgrade the current SAP ECC6 finance system to SAP S/4HANA - delivering modern, streamlined, and future‑ready finance capabilities. They are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong finance process and SAP knowledge to help drive business readiness and ensure a smooth transition to the upgraded platform. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complex environments, enjoys working across diverse finance and ICT teams, and is passionate about delivering high‑quality outcomes in government settings.

Your new role


As the Business Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the S/4HANA upgrade by:

Analysing current SAP ECC6 finance processes and contributing to the design of future‑state processes in S/4HANA.

Assisting with configuration reviews, process re‑engineering, and finance data mapping.
Supporting data migration activities, including data cleansing, validation, and reconciliation with both finance and ICT teams.
Preparing high‑quality documentation such as business process models, requirements specifications, and user stories.
Participating in design workshops and walkthroughs to ensure business needs are clearly understood and incorporated.
Developing test plans, coordinating user acceptance testing, and supporting defect management from a finance perspective.
Contributing to training material development and broader change management activities.
Maintaining accurate, auditable documentation aligned with Queensland Government project governance standards.
Identifying and escalating project risks, issues, and dependencies.

What you'll need to succeed


Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst on finance or SAP-related projects.
Experience working with SAP ECC6, S/4HANA, or similar ERP finance environments.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to document processes, requirements, and user stories in a clear and structured manner.
Experience in UAT preparation and execution.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, particularly with finance teams and technical delivery partners.
Knowledge of government finance standards (highly desirable).

What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call now (Himanshu Gupta - +61 (07) 3243 3014
If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

Job Title: Snr Technical and Functional Analyst D365 Finance
Ad Text:
About us:
Snackbrands Australia is part of Intersnack Group, a private German snack food company founded in 1968. Although internationally owned we are still very much a local company Creating Happy Snacking Moments for everyone!


Here at Snack Brands Australia, we proudly make some of Australia’s favourite snacks including Kettle, CC’s, Thins, Cheezels, The Natural Chip Company and Nice & Natural. Across our three sites, we make over 200 million packets of chips a year - that's a lot of enjoyment and fun we bring to snackers every day! We encourage a culture that is exciting, challenging and we strive for innovation in everything we do. Our aim is for our people to share in our success, enjoy our products and reap the benefits of their hard work within a supportive environment where work life balance is encouraged!


About the role:


We’re seeking an experienced Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 - Finance to join our IT Applications team and lead the delivery of D365 Finance projects, enhancements and support for our Finance function. If you’re a strong communicator who thrives on solving complex financial system challenges, this role is your opportunity to make a real impact.

This role will be based at our head office in Norwest with regular travel to our manufacturing site at Orchard Hills


What you’ll be responsible for:
Leading the process and system changes required in Dynamics 365 F&O, to streamline workflows and meet specific business requirements.
Develop critical project artifacts, including Business Requirement Specifications (BRS), Functional Design Documents (FDD), technical requirements and process flows.
Reduce the reliance on legacy systems, manual and XLS based processes by leveraging more Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations functionality.
Conduct Dynamics F&O working sessions with stakeholders to define requirements, demonstrate system capabilities, data migration and reporting to meet business requirements.
Partner with Finance, Production, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless, optimised and compliant end-to-end data flows and reporting.
Develop, execute and lead unit, system and user testing as required to implement changes into production
Support Finance users including production problems, incidents and change requests i.e. manage and resolve open tickets in your support queue.
Follow ITIL processes and methodologies for approval and migration of changes to production.
Work with vendors to provide support and upgrade information.
Analyse, troubleshoot and resolve issues across Microsoft D365 Finance modules, collaborating with IT and business stakeholders.
Deliver Finance related D365 projects and enhancements on budget and aligned to business goals.
Work closely with Finance leaders to ensure solutions meet operational and compliance requirements.
Build strong relationships across business functions and positively influence best-practice solution design.
Ensure compliance with broader IT strategy and architecture.



What you’ll bring to the role:
Proven experience as a Senior BA – Finance, ideally within FMCG.
Excellent Dynamics 365 and business process knowledge in Finance and Operations – able to configure modules and integrate to other legacy applications.
Solution knowledge of Dynamics 365, SQL data base and integration technologies.
Ability to research and facilitate D365 application solutions to meet business requirements
Excellent knowledge of quality assurance and security related to D365 applications.
Strong stakeholder management, negotiation and communication skills.
Ability to prepare business cases, estimates and project documentation.
Strong analytical, commercial and systems-thinking capability.
Accounting and financial management education or experience an advantage.
Experience managing multiple priorities across functions with conflicting requirements.
Knowledge of high-volume transaction and billing systems is an advantage.


What can you expect in return?
A supportive working environment (with plenty of snacks on hand!) where you'll feel engaged and valued, career growth, Birthday Leave, Access to discounts on Retailers, onsite parking (including electric car charging stations) and a focus on health and wellness (like Wellbeing Days!) to make your workplace more enjoyable!




**Our application process includes an Application Form that will be emailed to you directly after your submission. Please ensure you check your junk inbox for this application form, as only applications with this form will be considered. Thank you

We are partnering with a leading financial services organisation undertaking a large-scale Financial Crime transformation program focused on strengthening AML/CTF controls, governance frameworks and operational processes.

As part of this program, we are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support the delivery of AML/CTF Reform initiatives, working across business, risk, operations and technology teams to assess impacts, define requirements and support implementation across the organisation.

This is an excellent opportunity to work on a high-profile regulatory program with significant stakeholder exposure across a complex enterprise environment.

Role Overview:

In this role you will support business impact assessments and discovery workstreams across multiple business units, helping ensure AML/CTF policy changes are effectively translated into operational and technology outcomes.

You will work closely with stakeholders across risk, compliance, operations, product and technology teams to facilitate workshops, document requirements and support governance and reporting across the program.

Key Responsibilities:

* Conduct detailed analysis of AML/CTF policy changes and assess operational impacts across business units
* Lead discovery activities including gap analysis, process mapping and data impact assessments
* Facilitate workshops and stakeholder engagement sessions to gather and validate requirements
* Produce high quality documentation, artefacts, reports and presentations for program stakeholders
* Ensure traceability across impacted processes and requirements
* Support governance, reporting and compliance documentation across the AML/CTF program
* Maintain delivery timelines and assist in coordinating activities across multiple transformation initiatives
* Communicate regularly with stakeholders across risk, compliance, operations and technology teams

Skills and Experience:

* Proven experience as a Business Analyst within financial services or banking
* Strong knowledge of AML/CTF frameworks, policy and regulatory environments
* Experience delivering impact assessments, process mapping and gap analysis
* Ability to engage effectively with senior stakeholders across business and technology teams
* Strong documentation, analysis and problem-solving skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Desirable Experience:

* Exposure to AML technology platforms such as Fenergo, NetReveal or similar
* Experience working on large regulatory or transformation programs
* Background in financial crime, compliance or risk functions

Why Apply

* Opportunity to work on a major financial crime transformation program
* High visibility role with enterprise stakeholders
* Influence regulatory uplift and operational change initiatives
* Hybrid working environment based in Sydney

If you are an experienced Business Analyst with AML/CTF experience looking to contribute to a large regulatory transformation program, we would like to hear from you.

Make a meaningful difference with us:

We want people who are great as individuals, and even better in a team.

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

 

What you will bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Commitment to the mission and values of the not-for-profit sector.

 

Why you will love it here:

Working with us means stepping into a role that’s as broad as it is meaningful. You will gain exposure to:

Multiple industries under one roof - health, aged care, disability, community services, and more.

A wide range of workforce models, leadership styles, and organisational challenges.

Opportunities to stretch, grow, and shape your career in ways you won’t find elsewhere.

 

And the perks? We’ve got plenty:

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.

Access to salary packaging, meal and entertainment benefits - up to $15,900 per year tax free as well as up to $2,650 of your pre-tax income to pay for meal and entertainment benefits. To learn more about this benefit watch the video via the following link: Salary Packaging Explained.     

A recognition and rewards platform, providing employee discounts at hundreds of retailers, including everyday expenses, holidays, health insurance, a wellbeing program and employee assistance program.

Career development opportunities to challenge yourself, grow and make a meaningful difference.

12 weeks paid parental leave and 2 weeks paid partner leave, available after 6 months service, in addition to the government scheme.

Diversity and inclusive leave, offering paid gender affirmation leave and cultural leave options (paid and unpaid) for those who may celebrate Lunar New Year, Diwali, Ramadan, NAIDOC weeks and other significant cultural events.

We’re committed to creating safe, inclusive, and welcoming environments for all—especially children, young people, and the elderly. Our services are built on trust, care, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Ready to bring your whole self to work and make a real difference? Apply now and join a team where your work truly matters.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Design and implement data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Technology One), HRIS (e.g., Workday, UKG), payroll systems (e.g., Employment Hero, MicrOpay, Payroll Metrics), and enterprise automation platforms.
Develop and maintain data integrations with external financial systems including banking platforms (e.g., payment gateways, direct debit services, reconciliation tools) and taxation systems (e.g., ATO, GST reporting, Single Touch Payroll, PAYG).
Build and manage data flows from recruitment platforms such as LinkedIn, SEEK, and other talent acquisition systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights through dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance across all integrated systems.
Monitor and optimize data workflows for performance and reliability.
Maintain comprehensive documentation for data architecture, mappings, and technical specifications.
Work closely with cloud and architecture teams to deploy and manage data solutions in Databricks and Power BI.

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program 

 

 

What Sets You Apart 

Strong experience in data engineering and analytics, including ETL processes and data modelling.
Proven track record working with ERP, HR, payroll, finance, and recruitment data.
Familiarity with banking data structures, payment processing, and financial reconciliation.
Experience with taxation data and compliance reporting (e.g., Single Touch Payroll, BAS, PAYG).
Hands-on experience with payroll data and understanding of regulatory requirements.
Proficiency in SQL and programming languages such as Python, R, or Scala.
Experience with AWS data services including Glue and EventBridge.
Experience with data platforms like Snowflake or Databricks.
Understanding of secure data handling protocols and privacy standards.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect we’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and we embrace diversity.  

 

This role is available to candidates with unlimited Australian working rights only. Sponsorship is not available.  

 

Compare the Market and iSelect are committed to protecting your privacy. All applications and personal information are kept confidential and securely stored in line with Australian privacy laws, used only for recruitment purposes, and not shared with third parties outside the Auto and General Holdings Group without your consent.

Data Migration Lead

Asset Management Transformation

Initial 12 Month Fixed-Term Contract

About the Role

Due to significant global growth, Kinetic are undertaking a large scale, multi stream program of work designed to transform our technology landscape, create simplified, consistent ways of working, and ensure our future growth plans are underpinned by seamless technology products and processes. 

This role oversees the end-to-end migration lifecycle including scope definition, extraction, transformation, loading (ETL), validation, reconciliation, defect management, and cutover planning. The Data Migration Lead will work closely with internal analysts, business SMEs, Data Governance Leads, SI Partners, and system owners to ensure high-quality, fit-for-purpose data is available at go-live and beyond.

What You Will Do
Lead end‑to‑end data migration delivery across our Asset Management transformation.
Drive data extraction, cleansing, validation, and reconciliation activities.
Manage migration plans, schedules, mock loads, risks, and stakeholder alignment.
Build and maintain source‑to‑target mappings, transformation rules, and documentation.
Collaborate with IT, architects, SMEs, and SI partners to ensure readiness and data quality.
Apply strong SQL, advanced Excel, and data validation skills within large transformation programs.
What You Will Need
5+ years in data migration, including 2+ years in a leadership role.
Proven experience delivering complex multi‑system migrations
Strong understanding of ETL processes, data pipelines, and migration tools.
Skilled in coordinating across business and technical teams to manage requirements, timelines, and risks.
Hands‑on capability with SQL, advanced Excel, and data validation.
Experience in large‑scale transformation or system implementation programs.
Relevant tertiary qualification in Information Systems, Data Management, or a related field.
About us:

At Kinetic, we don't just move people - we're creating the positive change our planet needs. As a leader in sustainable transport, we're committed to creating cleaner, safer, and greener environments for generations to come.

Our size and scale give our people real opportunities to learn, grow, and progress in their careers. Calm, positive and respectful team players thrive here. Our people embrace change and are encouraged to stretch their comfort zone while making a difference. We challenge each other to be our best.

We're driven by respect, diversity and genuine connection. From networking groups to community partnerships and volunteering, our camaraderie naturally brings people together. Our energy and dedication keep communities moving and our people growing.

How to apply:

Kinetic is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and our people represent the community which we service. We invite all suitably qualified applicants to apply, including First Nations People, and people from diverse social, cultural and gender backgrounds.

If you're interested in this rewarding role with Kinetic then click the 'Apply' button now!

Job Title: Senior Tech Business Analyst - AI Transformation
Ad Text:
Senior Technical Business Analyst – AI Transformation

Drive real business outcomes using AI

We are currently delivering a strategic AI transformation program focused on generating measurable business outcomes across operations — including cost reduction, revenue growth and productivity improvement.

We are seeking a Senior Technical Business Analyst to play a key role in shaping and delivering these initiatives.

This role sits at the centre of the transformation — working directly with business leaders, architects and engineering teams to identify opportunities, define solutions and ensure initiatives deliver real, measurable value.

This is not a documentation-only BA role. We are looking for someone who can challenge thinking, quantify value and drive outcomes.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Driving AI Use Cases from Idea to Delivery

You will help identify, shape and deliver AI-driven initiatives that generate tangible business benefits.

Your work will include:

• Identifying opportunities where AI and automation can deliver business value
• Defining clear success metrics (cost savings, revenue uplift, productivity gains)
• Developing strong business cases to support investment decisions
• Translating business problems into technical solutions with architects and engineers

Business Case & Value Definition

You will build structured business cases that clearly define the value of each initiative.

This includes:

• Business value definition
• Cost-benefit modelling
• Measurement frameworks for:

Cost savings

Revenue improvement

Productivity gains
• Post-delivery validation of outcomes

Requirements & Solution Definition

You will lead the documentation and validation of requirements across initiatives.

Deliverables will include:

• Functional Requirements (Level 1–3)
• Non-Functional Requirements
• Context diagrams
• Process flows and swim lane diagrams
• User journeys
• Operational support documentation

You will work closely with solution architects and engineering teams to ensure requirements translate cleanly into delivery.

Delivery & Testing Support

• Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
• Support Production Verification Testing
• Validate solutions against expected business outcomes
• Ensure operational teams are equipped with support documentation

What We Are Looking For

We are looking for someone who can think commercially and operate technically.

You will likely come from consulting, enterprise transformation or large technology delivery environments.

Experience

• 5–10+ years experience as a Business Analyst / Technical BA
• Experience working on complex technology or transformation initiatives
• Strong experience building business cases and value frameworks
• Experience working closely with solution architects and engineering teams

Technical Capability

You should be comfortable translating business problems into technical requirements.

Experience with:
• AI, automation or data-driven solutions (highly desirable)
• End-to-end SDLC environments
• Process modelling and solution design support

Tools

Strong proficiency in:

• Jira
• Confluence
• Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio)

The Type of Person Who Succeeds in This Role

You are someone who:

• Can cut through ambiguity and structure complex problems
• Can challenge stakeholders constructively
• Is comfortable working in fast-moving delivery environments
• Can balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
• Is able to work autonomously and drive outcomes

Location & Working Model

• Melbourne based
• Hybrid working model (remote only is not optional)




Why This Role is Interesting

You will:

• Work on real enterprise AI initiatives, not just theory
• Influence large operational and commercial decisions
• Work directly with senior business stakeholders and engineering teams
• Deliver solutions that impact thousands of customers and employees

Application Process

As part of the selection process, candidates may be asked to demonstrate examples of previous work including:

• Business cases
• Requirements documentation
• Process flows or user journeys
• UAT or delivery artefacts

If you are a Technical Business Analyst who wants to work on high-impact AI initiatives rather than routine BA work, we would like to hear from you.

Apply now via Seek.

Job Title: Finance Analyst / FP&A and M&A / Sydney CBD
Ad Text:
Finance Analyst / FP&A and M&A / Sydney CBD 

Sydney CBD location
Big 4 audit–trained professionals encouraged
Global business with genuine career progression
This is a standout opportunity for a Big 4–trained accountant to step into a commercially focused role within a large, internationally operating organisation.

Sitting within a lean global finance and corporate development function, you’ll work closely with senior finance leadership and gain exposure to high-value decision-making across forecasting, reporting, and corporate development initiatives. This is a role that goes well beyond traditional accounting — offering real visibility, autonomy, and influence.

The Role Reporting to a senior global finance leader, you’ll combine technical rigour with commercial analysis, supporting executive-level decisions across multiple regions.

Key responsibilities:

Monthly management reporting, forecasting and variance analysis
Group-level budgeting and analytical review
Ad-hoc financial and commercial analysis for senior stakeholders
Responding to complex queries from global head office and external parties
Liaising with external auditors and providing technical accounting support
Supporting M&A, corporate development and investment activity
Building a deep understanding of operational and financial value drivers
This is an ideal move for someone looking to transition from core reporting into broader commercial and strategic exposure.

About You
This role suits a high-calibre accountant with strong Big 4 foundations and a desire to build a career in commercial finance and corporate development.
You’ll ideally bring:

Big 4 audit training (essential)
CA / CPA qualification (or equivalent)
Exposure to Deals, Transaction Services, Valuations, Investment Banking or Corporate Development (highly regarded)
Strong financial modelling, Excel and PowerPoint capability
Solid knowledge of IFRS and complex reporting
Sharp analytical thinking, commercial judgement and intellectual curiosity
Confidence operating in a fast-paced, high-accountability environment
If you’re ready to accelerate your career in a globally connected, commercially driven finance team, this is well worth exploring.

To register your interest, please submit your CV via the prompts.

For further information, contact Ed Kirby on 0419 615 720.

Job Title: Senior Manager, Data Science (Lead Data Scientist)
Ad Text:
We look forward to welcoming a Technical Leader who will lead projects with cutting-edge AI technologies and pave the way for innovative practices that benefit not only CommBank but the banking industry as a whole.

Do work that matters:

As part of Risk Management, you will participate in the development, testing, evaluation and deployment of models and AI solutions, to help Risk Management deliver on our Ambition, supporting the bank as we reimagine customer experiences and lead in AI.

See yourself in our team:

As a Lead Data Scientist, you will work with a team of data scientists and problem solvers using technologies and techniques from data science to drive decisions and risk outcomes.

Why CommBank?

CommBank is leading the world of banking by venturing into new-to-the-world innovations. We're on the cutting edge, embracing AI technologies to reshape how we operate and serve our customers. When you join CommBank, you’re joining a team that values expertise and innovation, and you’ll be empowered to lead projects that push the boundaries of what's possible.

Key Responsibilities:

• Demonstrate deep technical knowledge and specialist expertise to develop innovative solutions to complex technical problems, using advanced analytics techniques, including machine learning and natural language processing across large datasets.

• Generate and communicate insights aligned to business objectives and relevant to diverse stakeholders and clients, taking into consideration business roles and technical understanding of audiences.

• Lead and manage data scientists to achieve their best by creating a high-performance culture, providing guidance and coaching on appropriate modelling and statistical techniques.

• Provide strategic direction to the team and lead multiple projects to create scalable and reliable end-to-end solutions for our customers, prioritising with business stakeholders and balancing long-term objectives versus immediate delivery.

• Conduct extensive big data analysis, optimising data processing workflows, and deriving deeper insights from big data, while integrating big data solutions with cloud platforms.

• Design and implement more complex machine learning solutions, handling larger datasets, tuning models for better performance, and working on sophisticated problems.

• Apply practical experience in software engineering, participating in and contributing to software development projects, and ensuring best practices in coding standards, documentation, and code reviews.




We’re interested in hearing from people who have:

· Expertise in the design and construction of machine learning models, including feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, model evaluation, and deployment of predictive models into production environments.

· Familiarity with various aspects of Generative AI, including prompt engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), guardrail design, orchestration design, and tools such as LangChain and LangGraph.

· Worked on projects leveraging traditional AI, Generative AI, and Agentic AI to pave the path for how banking and broader industry functions in the future.

· Experience with version control and CI/CD pipelines such as GitHub.

· Familiarity with Docker for containerization and deployment of applications.

· Grounded understanding in AWS or Azure Cloud architecture and integration, demonstrating experience in deploying and managing cloud-based solutions.
